# Requirements Alignment Checklist: Internal Symbol and Normalization Backbone - M2B.2

**Purpose**: Pressure-test the M2B.2 specification and plan for requirement quality, SRS coverage, architecture alignment, ambiguity, and traceability before task generation.
**Created**: 2026-07-06
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements. It tests whether the requirements are written well enough for implementation planning; it does not test implementation behavior.

- [x] CHK001 Are all mapped `FR-2.5.1` through `FR-2.5.5` requirements represented by explicit envelope, normalized output, context-pack, raw-payload exclusion, and degraded-state requirements? [Completeness, Spec Â§Governance Context, Spec Â§M2B2-FR-011..015]

[Completeness, Spec Â§M2B2-FR-007, Contract Â§ProviderAdapterDescriptor]
- [x] CHK004 Are provider selection policy requirements complete for provider order, fallback eligibility, fail-closed conditions, market-session rules, freshness expectations, timeout budget, and degraded-state mapping? [Completeness, Spec Â§M2B2-FR-009, Contract Â§ProviderSelectionPolicy]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK008 Is the distinction between current M2B.1 behavior, target M2B.2 behavior, and downstream M2B.3/M2B.4 behavior clear enough to prevent status drift? [Clarity, Spec Â§Lifecycle Status Rule, Plan Â§Constraints]
- [x] CHK009 Is "internal symbol-store boundary" defined clearly enough to distinguish symbol identity from market-data facts? [Clarity, Spec Â§M2B2-FR-002..004, Data Model Â§InternalSymbolRecord]
- [x] CHK010 Is "provider-hidden below model-visible tools" stated with enough specificity to prevent flat provider lists or provider-specific tools from becoming model-visible? [Clarity, Spec Â§M2B2-FR-008, Plan Â§Constraints]
- [x] CHK011 Is "request-scoped `ToolContextPack`" defined with enough precision to distinguish prompt context from durable memory, retained artifacts, and market truth? [Clarity, Spec Â§M2B2-FR-013, Spec Â§M2B2-FR-017, Data Model Â§ToolContextPack]
- [x] CHK012 Are "raw payload" exclusions clear enough to cover raw provider data, raw web/PDF bytes, scripts, hidden page text, untrusted instructions, parser artifacts, credentials, and parser internals? [Clarity, Spec Â§M2B2-FR-014, Contract Â§Prompt Boundary Rules]
- [x] CHK013 Is the allowed meaning of "retained derivative" clear enough to avoid persisting full request context or raw source payloads? [Clarity, Spec Â§M2B2-FR-018, Contract Â§Retained Derivative Rules]
- [x] CHK014 Is "production eligibility" for providers defined clearly enough to require reviewed licensing, terms, credential scope, and redistribution posture? [Clarity, Spec Â§M2B2-FR-010, Contract Â§Admission Rules]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK029 Are primary scenarios documented for each P1 story: symbol identity, provider-hidden policy, output normalization, request-scoped retention, and disabled mutation receipts? [Coverage, Spec Â§User Scenarios & Testing]
- [x] CHK030 Are alternate scenarios documented for ambiguous symbols, no eligible provider, stale cache/provider state, and no-source retained derivatives? [Coverage, Spec Â§Edge Cases]

- [x] CHK033 Are no-tool or no-provider scenarios addressed without silently widening scope to downstream market-data tools? [Coverage, Spec Â§M2B2-FR-021, Plan Â§Scope]
- [x] CHK034 Are prompt-boundary scenarios covered for normalized facts, snippets, documents, system records, visualization provenance, generated artifacts, mutation receipts, and degraded states? [Coverage, Contract Â§Tool Execution Normalization]
- [x] CHK035 Are retention scenarios covered for allowed derivatives without whole-pack persistence? [Coverage, Spec Â§User Story 4, Data Model Â§RetainedDerivative]
- [x] CHK036 Are mutation scenarios covered for blocked/default, future policy representation, and test-only or future approved receipt shape? [Coverage, Spec Â§User Story 5]

## Notes

- Depth: Standard.
- Actor/timing: Reviewer-facing checklist before `/speckit-tasks`.
- Focus areas: M2B.2 SRS coverage, architecture/technical-design alignment, normalized tool context, provider-hidden policy, retained derivatives, mutation receipts, and downstream scope boundaries.
- Disposition: Closed during `/speckit-analyze` remediation on 2026-07-06 after aligning `plan.md`, `quickstart.md`, and `tasks.md` for retention/mutation tests, prompt-boundary integration, task evidence, and sync coverage.
